raw_result.description: Cloudflare provides APIs for managing Cloudflare services and resources across accounts and zones.
raw_result.auth_notes: Cloudflare’s API docs explicitly document API Tokens and legacy Global API Key authentication. The Global API Key is passed with X-Auth-Key plus X-Auth-Email headers, so it is mapped as other rather than token.
raw_result.gating_reason: Cloudflare provides publicly accessible API documentation and self-serve account sign-up/pricing pages; no partner approval or sales contact is indicated to start using the API.

api_surface.breadth: Broad platform API surface spanning REST endpoints for accounts, zones, DNS, security, workers, and more, plus a GraphQL API and related developer tooling.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Cloudflare publicly exposes an MCP offering, evidenced by its official cloudflare/mcp repository and docs navigation mentioning MCP servers.

raw_result.blocker: Auth is buildable today, but the exact structured evidence gathered in this pass does not conclusively show self-serve API credential creation and MCP scope from the pages read, so a cautious first toolkit may need manual credential setup confirmation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Cloudflare documents a global REST API limit of 1,200 requests per five minutes per user/account token, plus GraphQL limits and standard rate-limit response headers on its API limits reference page.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs indicate multiple API interfaces (REST and GraphQL), legacy and modern auth options, documented rate-limit headers, and public MCP-related resources.
raw_result.human_review_reason: This corrected pass was stopped before opening additional official pricing/auth pages to fully substantiate structured fields like access_tier and buildability_verdict from direct page content alone; current conclusions rely partly on broader platform inference from the docs set already gathered.

result.auth_notes: Cloudflare’s API docs explicitly document API Tokens and legacy Global API Key authentication. The Global API Key is passed with X-Auth-Key plus X-Auth-Email headers, so it is mapped as other rather than token.
result.gating_reason: Cloudflare provides publicly accessible API documentation and self-serve account sign-up/pricing pages; no partner approval or sales contact is indicated to start using the API.
